prompt:
I defenately agree with you, that university life is harder, than school. What about me, it was very dificult for me to study the first monthes, but then, I have invented some rules, which I follow every day, and my life become a bit easier. 

There are always a lot of tasks for several weeks or even monthes! I have failed first such tasks, because planned to do then at the last few days, and that was a really bad idea. The better option is to divide big task into smaller ones and create your own deadlines to do each of them.

Another problem was that nobody control me, that is why I begin to forget about my educational duties. I strated to keep a To-Do list, where I write about all my lessons, tasks and projects. I have also set up a notification system, which always remind me about everything I should do.

I believe, that if you will also manage you time well, you will achieve greater success.

Use EXPERT_ANNOTATION, CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, and RANKING_ANCHORS only as supporting context; never override direct evidence from the student text.
Return ONLY one CEFR label from: A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2.
Do not output JSON.
Do not output explanation.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: I defenately agree with you, that university life is harder, than school. What about me, it was very dificult for me to study the first monthes, but then, I have invented some rules, which I follow every day, and my life become a bit easier. 

There are always a lot of tasks for several weeks or even monthes! I have failed first such tasks, because planned to do then at the last few days, and that was a really bad idea. The better option is to divide big task into smaller ones and create your own deadlines to do each of them.

Another problem was that nobody control me, that is why I begin to forget about my educational duties. I strated to keep a To-Do list, where I write about all my lessons, tasks and projects. I have also set up a notification system, which always remind me about everything I should do.

I believe, that if you will also manage you time well, you will achieve greater success.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

So many students have the same problem, and me to. 
    In first semester it was wery big problem for me. But now I have some ideas how can I fix it. I will give you them.

1) You need to have some task-management system. In that system you can prioritize your tasks.

2) You study in uni, so you have some classes. Use "Google Calendar" to manage them.

3) Combine similar tasks - then you will do all tasks faster.

4) Remember to get enouth sleep and to do sport well, cause it can bust your productivity.

    I hope that some of my tips can help you. I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"HI! I had (and sometimes have) this feeling too. I think, that we have it because we can't organize the studying process properly. So, I've got some tips for you.

	* Firstly, create a TODO list. Write down tasks that you have to do. It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this. Also you can mark completed tasks. But the key tip for TODO list is not to write very big tasks, but to split them into the smaller ones. 
	* Have a BREAK. You can't concentrate on the task for a long time, your mind needs time to relax.

If you stick to this tips, you would't think all the time "I need so much to do", because you will be more productive and satisfied with the list of completed tasks.
